headline: 47 of 100 stores (47%) drive 80% of all exceptions
detail: 47 stores account for 1287 of 1603 exceptions (80%). Exception workload is 1.7x more concentrated than the store footprint. Fixing the top stores first yields outsized backlog reduction.
recommended_action: Assign a dedicated task force to the vital-few stores; run root-cause review per store before the next campaign phase.

headline: North region over-indexes 1.3x on low sell through
detail: North holds 23% of stores but generates 31% of low_sell_through exceptions (202 of 659). A 1.3x lift indicates a localized process gap rather than a systemic one.
recommended_action: Brief the North regional lead; audit the local workflow behind low sell through and retrain if needed.
evidence: 202/659 low_sell_through exceptions in North; store share 23%

headline: 8 SKUs in C-2026-BTS have both stockout and overstock stores - transfer candidates
detail: In campaign C-2026-BTS, 8 SKUs have 5 stores at stockout risk while 23 stores hold excess inventory of the same SKU. Store-to-store transfers can resolve both exposures without new intake or markdowns.
recommended_action: Generate a transfer shortlist pairing stockout and overstock stores per SKU; validate against transit time before the campaign window closes.
evidence: Top SKUs by combined exposure: BTS-008, BTS-007, BTS-003, BTS-005, BTS-009

headline: Field rep Nora Smith over-indexes 1.9x on proof and confirmation failures
detail: Rep Nora Smith covers 7% of stores but accounts for 13% of confirmation and photo-proof exceptions (47 of 354). This may indicate route overload, training needs, or coverage gaps.
recommended_action: Review Nora Smith's route load and visit schedule; rebalance store coverage or provide refresher training on proof submission.
